evals.prompt: I'm starting a new Hugo site for a C++ course I'm teaching. I need to set it up with the Zen theme, version 0.161.0. The site will have modules, homework assignments, and resources. What are the initialization steps and how should I structure my content directories?
evals.expected_output: Complete initialization walkthrough including: git setup, theme installation as submodule, hugo.toml configuration with course-specific taxonomies (difficulty, agent, topic), and directory structure for /content/modules, /content/homework, /content/resources

evals.prompt: I'm creating homework assignments for my course and need to set up the front matter consistently. Each assignment needs: title, difficulty level (beginner/intermediate/advanced), related agents (memory, reasoning, planning), C++ topics covered, and a due date. Also, I want to attach PDFs and code files to assignments. How should I structure this with Hugo?
evals.expected_output: YAML front matter template showing all fields, explanation of custom taxonomies configuration, leaf bundle structure for attaching resources (PDFs, code files), and how to reference those resources in templates

evals.prompt: I need guidance on organizing my course modules using Hugo bundles. Some modules have multiple lessons underneath them, and I want to keep resources like code examples and diagrams with their corresponding lessons. What's the difference between section bundles and leaf bundles, and how should I apply them?
evals.expected_output: Clear explanation of section bundles (with _index.md) vs leaf bundles (with index.md), practical examples for your course structure, directory layout showing modules with lessons, and how to organize resources without CSS getting too complex
